bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 with no prior opponent offers or messages, I am targeting my maximum-utility opening bid. Since there is no evidence yet about opponent priorities, I will start by holding all issues at my most preferred value and wait for their counteroffer to reveal tradeoffs.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02), and the opponent’s only offer was the minimum-value bid for me on every issue. To avoid simply repeating my opening bid while making only a small concession, I concede on low-weight issueA and hold firm on the more important issues, especially issueF, issueE, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the all-valueA offer, which is very poor for me and shows no concession so far; with no messages to consider, I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while avoiding repeating my last exact bid.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a meaningful concession on my lowest-weight issues. The opponent has moved from all valueA to allowing issueD=valueC and issueE/issueF=valueB, but they still seem to prefer valueA on several issues, so I concede issueA and issueB while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.08, while acknowledging the opponent has already moved substantially toward my preferred values on issueD, issueE, and issueF. Their latest offer suggests they may tolerate valueC on the high-weight issues, so I will hold firm there and only concede mostly on low-weight issueA while making a small ask on issueB to avoid simply repeating my prior bid.

The opponent has clearly conceded from all valueA to a much better offer for me (issueA/valueA, issueB/valueA, issueC/valueB, and valueC on the high-weight issues D/E/F), but at t=0.10 it is still early enough to hold for a modest improvement. I am targeting a high-utility bid by accepting their concessions on low-weight issueA/issueB while asking them to improve issueC to valueC, preserving my strongest gains on issuesD-F.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making concessions only on low-weight issues A and B. The opponent has moved away from all-valueA offers and has shown willingness to give valueC on D and E, sometimes F, so I am holding firm on the high-importance issues C, D, E, and F.

I am targeting a high-utility bid while conceding only on low-weight issues, since the time is still early at t=0.14 and there is no need for major concessions yet. The opponent has clearly moved from all valueA toward accepting valueC on my important issues C, D, E, and F, so I will hold those firm and test whether they can also tolerate valueB on issueB while giving them issueA.

I am targeting a high utility bid for me (about 0.94), conceding only on the two lowest-weight issues while holding valueC on the more important issues C, D, E, and F. Although it is still early, the opponent has repeatedly offered this exact valid package at t=0.14 and t=0.16, so matching it is likely to secure agreement without sacrificing much utility.
